HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TIJI THOMAS's Motion For Relief From Default And/Or Default Judgment

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Tiji Thomas's "motion for relief from default and/or default judgment" is granted.

"[I]t is now well-acknowledged that an attorney has an ethical obligation to warn opposing counsel that the attorney is about to take an adversary's default." (Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135 [emphasis original].)

If Thomas was mistaken about filing another answer, relief was timely sought, and it is California courts' strong policy to resolve disputes on their merits.

Order signed.

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHEILA GROPPER NELSON, Esq. of Resolution Law Firm P.C. (Tel: 415-362-2221) for plaintiff;
Atty. ZACHARY C. FRAMPTON, Esq. of Holland & Knight LLP (Tel: 213-896-2400) for defendant Barclays Bank Delaware.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE'S DEMURRER.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Barclays Bank Delaware's "demurrer to plaintiff Yolanda Conn's first amended complaint" (FAC) is sustained in part and overruled in part.
1 (breach of contract): Contrary to Barclays' notice of demurrer, the FAC adequately pleads a written contract, that plaintiff performed her contractual obligations and breach. (Not. 1:15-18; FAC 6:5-8, 31:1-22.) Barclays' notion that a contract claim must be pled with specificity is mistaken. (See Not. 1:15-18.) Demurrer overruled.
2 (fraud [concealment]), 3 (intentional misrepresentation), 7 (Business & Professions Code 17200): The FAC pleads fraud with insufficient specificity as to Barclays. (Not. 1:19-2:2; Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73 ["how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered"].) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.
4: (conversion): Plaintiff' pleads that her "good credit" was converted, but the cause of action requires a tangible interest. (FAC 34:15-16; Not. 2:3-9; Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 120.) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.
5 (elder abuse): Barclays says this claim "does not lie against companies providing ordinary services," but the FAC pleads more than that. (Not. 2:10-13.) Demurrer overruled.
6 (intentional infliction of emotional distress): As Barclays says, the FAC does not adequately allege distress-causing conduct by it. (Not. 2:14-18.) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.
8 (unjust enrichment): The better view is that unjust enrichment is a remedy, not a cause of action. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
